description: Irreplaceable app state on a named volume silently escapes Kopia's bind-mount scope — must be flagged.
input:
Review this new service's storage:
```
# ansible/roles/containers/notesdb/templates/docker-compose.yml.j2
services:
  notesdb:
    image: postgres:16.3
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=notes
    volumes:
      - notesdb_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped
volumes:
  notesdb_data:
```
Context: notesdb holds the only copy of the user's notes app database. Repo convention: Kopia backs up bind mounts under containers/; named volumes fall outside that backup scope.
rubric: PASS only if the review flags that notesdb_data is a named volume holding irreplaceable, non-regenerable data that is not covered by backups (it falls outside Kopia's bind-mount scope), and recommends a bind mount under containers/ or explicit backup coverage. Must NOT wave it through as an accepted named-volume exception — this data is not regenerable and carries no justifying comment.
